headline: The Wonderdogs Just Went Full Mike Tyson on General Hospital
subheadline: A 9-0 shutout, two ties that felt like kissing your sister, and the Curd Fryers experiencing their cheese curd nightmare in Week 13.
body:
Look, I've been doing this long enough to know when a team makes a statement. And **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** didn't just make a statement against **General Hospital** — they walked into the ER, unplugged every machine, and left a note that said 'We'll see you in October.' A 9-0 shutout. NINE TO ZERO. That's not a baseball score, that's a Cobra Kai beatdown. That's Ivan Drago looking into the camera and saying 'I must break you' except he actually did it. **General Hospital** came in thinking they were McDreamy and left looking like a patient on a gurney getting wheeled into the morgue.

(Side note: Has any team ever recovered from a 9-0 loss with their dignity intact? I'm genuinely asking. This felt like watching someone's dad try to dance at a wedding — you want to look away but you just... can't.)

Meanwhile, in the 'games that actually resembled competitive baseball' department, the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** handled **North Correa** 6-4 in what I'd call a 'professional victory.' Nothing flashy. Just good fundamentals. Very Tim Duncan energy. Very 'I'm going to beat you, shake your hand, then go home and play Dungeons & Dragons' vibes.

The **Albuquerque Bombers** demolished the **Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers** 7-2, which honestly feels like cosmic justice because I refuse to trust any team named after deep-fried dairy. (You had this coming, Curd Fryers. You knew it. We all knew it.)

Can we talk about these ties for a second? **Benchwarmers** and **Athletic Park Dragons** finished 5-5. **Hiroshima Radiation** and **Sugar Land Skeeters** also went 5-5. Four teams just collectively agreed to accomplish nothing. It's like that episode of *The Office* where everyone pretends to work but nobody actually does anything productive. Congratulations, you all played yourselves.

Now, here's where things get weird — the standings are showing everyone at 0-0 with zero points, which either means our stats guy is having a stroke or we're witnessing some Matrix-level glitch in the simulation. (I'm choosing to believe it's the latter because it's more fun.)

What I DO know is this: **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** are playing like a team possessed, **BaseballPro77** quietly keeps winning (8-2 over **Buttville Skidmarks**, a team whose name I still can't say on my podcast), and the playoff race is about to get VERY interesting.

Next week? Buckle up. The pretenders are about to get exposed.
